notes: L374F variant in SLC45A2 melanin transporter gene. Major effect in European populations. The A allele frequency is >95% in Europeans, <5% in sub-Saharan Africans.

AA.description: Homozygous LRRK2 G2019S, very high lifetime Parkinson's disease risk

AG.description: LRRK2 G2019S carrier, ~30-75% lifetime risk of Parkinson's disease (age-dependent penetrance)

GG.description: Wild-type LRRK2, population-average Parkinson's disease risk

description: LRRK2 G2019S is the most common genetic cause of familial Parkinson's disease, causing gain-of-function kinase activity that damages dopaminergic neurons.
notes: LRRK2 G2019S is found in ~1% of sporadic and 4% of familial Parkinson's cases. Penetrance is age-dependent (~30% by age 60, ~75% by age 80). Common in Ashkenazi Jewish and North African Berber populations.

CC.description: Normal LRP5/Wnt signaling, standard bone mineral density regulation

CT.description: Mildly impaired Wnt signaling, slightly lower bone mineral density

TT.description: Impaired LRP5/Wnt signaling, lower bone mineral density and increased fracture risk

description: LRP5 A1330V variant impairs the Wnt signaling co-receptor, reducing osteoblast activity and bone formation. A key genetic determinant of bone mineral density.
notes: LRP5 is a Wnt pathway co-receptor essential for bone formation. Gain-of-function mutations cause high bone mass, while loss-of-function causes osteoporosis-pseudoglioma syndrome. Val1330 reduces Wnt signaling ~15%.

CC.description: Enhanced hippocampal memory consolidation, better performance on episodic memory tasks

CT.description: Intermediate memory performance, moderate hippocampal activation

TT.description: Reduced hippocampal efficiency during memory tasks, lower episodic memory scores

description: KIBRA (WWC1) variant affects memory consolidation through its role in synaptic plasticity and long-term potentiation in the hippocampus.
notes: KIBRA interacts with PKMzeta and synaptopodin, proteins critical for long-term potentiation. Memory effect has been replicated across multiple cohorts. The T allele associated with less efficient hippocampal activation on fMRI.

AA.description: Protective genotype, reduced risk of pseudoexfoliation syndrome

AG.description: Intermediate risk for pseudoexfoliation syndrome and secondary glaucoma

GG.description: Risk genotype for pseudoexfoliation syndrome and exfoliation glaucoma

description: LOXL1 G153D variant is the second major LOXL1 polymorphism associated with pseudoexfoliation syndrome. Works in combination with rs1048661 to determine exfoliation glaucoma risk.
notes: G153D in LOXL1 exon 1 affects elastic fiber formation. Risk allele shows population-specific effects — the risk allele in one population can be protective in another. Exfoliation glaucoma accounts for ~25% of glaucoma worldwide.

CC.description: Full DPD enzyme activity, standard 5-fluorouracil chemotherapy dosing appropriate

CT.description: ~50% DPD activity, increased risk of severe 5-FU toxicity, dose reduction recommended

TT.description: Complete DPD deficiency, 5-fluorouracil can cause life-threatening toxicity including neutropenia and mucositis

description: DPYD IVS14+1G>A (DPYD*2A) splice site variant eliminates dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase activity, causing potentially fatal toxicity from 5-fluorouracil and capecitabine chemotherapy.
notes: DPYD*2A causes exon 14 skipping. Carrier frequency ~1% in Europeans. DPD degrades >80% of administered 5-FU. Pre-treatment DPYD testing is now recommended/mandated in EU and increasingly in US.

CC.description: Increased TERT-CLPTM1L expression, longer telomeres but higher skin cancer susceptibility

CT.description: Moderate telomere maintenance, intermediate skin cancer risk

TT.description: Lower TERT-CLPTM1L activity, slightly shorter telomeres but reduced skin cancer susceptibility

description: TERT-CLPTM1L 5p15.33 variant influences telomere length and has pleiotropic effects on multiple cancer types, particularly basal cell carcinoma and melanoma.
notes: 5p15.33 locus contains both TERT and CLPTM1L genes. Pleiotropic cancer risk — the same allele can increase risk for some cancers while decreasing risk for others. Major skin cancer susceptibility locus.

CC.description: Impaired hepatic statin uptake, 15-20x increased risk of simvastatin-induced myopathy

CT.description: Reduced hepatic statin uptake, ~5x increased risk of statin myopathy

TT.description: Normal OATP1B1 transporter function, standard statin myopathy risk

description: SLCO1B1*5 (V174A) reduces hepatic uptake of statins via the OATP1B1 transporter, increasing plasma statin levels and risk of myopathy. FDA-relevant pharmacogenomic marker.
notes: SLCO1B1*5 carrier frequency ~15% in Europeans. The SEARCH trial showed CC genotype had 16.9% vs 0.6% myopathy rate on simvastatin 80mg. CPIC recommends avoiding simvastatin 80mg in C allele carriers.

AA.description: Insertion/Insertion genotype, lowest ACE levels, endurance advantage, lower blood pressure

AG.description: Insertion/Deletion genotype, intermediate ACE levels and athletic profile

GG.description: Deletion/Deletion genotype, highest ACE levels, power/strength advantage, higher blood pressure tendency

description: ACE insertion/deletion polymorphism is the primary determinant of circulating ACE levels. The D allele doubles ACE activity, affecting blood pressure, athletic performance, and cardiac remodeling.
notes: This is a proxy SNP for the classic ACE I/D (287bp Alu insertion/deletion). II genotype prevalent in endurance athletes, DD in power athletes. D allele also associated with hypertension and left ventricular hypertrophy.

AA.description: Modified OCA2 expression favoring green eye color, particularly in light-eyed individuals

AG.description: Intermediate OCA2 modification, subtle influence toward green eye tones

GG.description: Standard OCA2 expression at this position, baseline eye color contribution

description: OCA2 regulatory variant that modifies eye color, particularly associated with green eye color phenotype. Works as part of the multi-gene eye color determination system.
notes: Green eye color is genetically complex, involving multiple loci. This OCA2 variant contributes to the green eye phenotype alongside HERC2, IRF4, and other pigmentation genes. Green eyes are found in ~2% of the global population.

AA.description: Non-functional CYP2C19, severely impaired metabolism of clopidogrel, PPIs, and antidepressants

AG.description: One *3 allele, reduced CYP2C19 activity, consider alternative drugs or dose adjustment

GG.description: No *3 allele at this position, normal CYP2C19 metabolizer status

description: CYP2C19*3 introduces a premature stop codon (W212X), completely eliminating enzyme activity. Most common loss-of-function allele in East Asian populations.
notes: CYP2C19*3 is rare in Europeans (<1%) but found in 5-10% of East Asians. Combined with *2, these are the two most important CYP2C19 loss-of-function alleles for clopidogrel pharmacogenomics.

AA.description: Impaired LRP5 function, lower bone mineral density and increased fracture risk

AG.description: Mildly impaired Wnt/LRP5 signaling, slightly lower bone mineral density

GG.description: Normal LRP5/Wnt co-receptor function, standard bone mineral density

description: LRP5 variant affecting the Wnt signaling pathway co-receptor. LRP5 is critical for osteoblast differentiation and bone formation, and variants directly influence bone mineral density.
notes: LRP5 mutations span a phenotypic spectrum from high bone mass (gain-of-function) to osteoporosis-pseudoglioma syndrome (loss-of-function). This variant has modest effect on bone density in the general population.

CC.description: Modified OR7D4 receptor, androstenone perceived as pleasant or undetectable

CT.description: Mixed perception, androstenone has intermediate intensity

TT.description: Fully functional OR7D4, androstenone perceived as intensely unpleasant 'sweaty/urinous'

description: OR7D4 olfactory receptor variant determines perception of androstenone, a steroid pheromone found in pork (boar taint), sweat, and truffles. Classic example of human olfactory genetic variation.
notes: OR7D4 R88W variant dramatically alters androstenone perception. ~50% of people find androstenone offensive, ~35% cannot smell it, and ~15% find it pleasant — largely determined by OR7D4 genotype.

AC.description: CHEK2 1100delC carrier, ~2-3x increased breast cancer risk, elevated colon and prostate cancer risk

AA.description: Homozygous CHEK2 1100delC, substantially increased risk of multiple cancer types

CC.description: Wild-type CHEK2, population-average cancer susceptibility

description: CHEK2 1100delC is a frameshift mutation that truncates checkpoint kinase 2, a critical DNA damage response protein. Moderate-penetrance multi-cancer susceptibility allele.
notes: CHEK2 1100delC is a moderate-penetrance cancer allele particularly prevalent in Northern/Eastern Europeans (~1-2%). Confers ~2-3x breast cancer risk. Also increases prostate, colorectal, and thyroid cancer risk. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Gain-of-function SCN9A variant, lower pain threshold, heightened nociception

AG.description: Heterozygous SCN9A variant, mildly modified pain perception

GG.description: Standard SCN9A function, population-average pain sensitivity

description: SCN9A encodes the Nav1.7 sodium channel, a key pain signaling molecule. Variants cause phenotypes ranging from congenital insensitivity to pain to extreme pain disorders.
notes: SCN9A/Nav1.7 is a validated analgesic drug target. Loss-of-function mutations cause congenital insensitivity to pain, while gain-of-function causes erythromelalgia and paroxysmal extreme pain disorder.

CC.description: Normal BDNF secretion, efficient hippocampal plasticity, better episodic memory

CT.description: Reduced activity-dependent BDNF secretion, slightly lower hippocampal efficiency

TT.description: Impaired activity-dependent BDNF secretion, reduced hippocampal-dependent memory

description: BDNF Val66Met (rs6265) affects activity-dependent secretion of brain-derived neurotrophic factor, the most important neurotrophin for hippocampal learning and memory.
notes: Val66Met impairs intracellular BDNF trafficking and activity-dependent secretion, but not constitutive secretion. Met carriers show reduced hippocampal volume, impaired episodic memory, and increased anxiety-related behavior.

GG.description: Homozygous 8q24 risk variant, ~1.5x increased colorectal and prostate cancer risk

GT.description: Heterozygous, ~1.2x increased colorectal and prostate cancer risk

TT.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average colorectal and prostate cancer risk

description: 8q24 variant near the MYC oncogene enhances a long-range enhancer element. The most consistently replicated common genetic risk factor for both colorectal and prostate cancer.
notes: 8q24 is a gene desert, but this variant sits in an enhancer element that physically contacts the MYC promoter 335kb away via chromatin looping. Risk allele frequency ~50%. TCF4 binding site affected by the variant. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CT.description: TREM2 R47H carrier, ~2-4x increased late-onset Alzheimer's disease risk via impaired microglial function

CC.description: Normal TREM2 function, standard microglial phagocytic capacity

TT.description: Homozygous TREM2 R47H, significantly impaired microglial function and elevated Alzheimer's risk

description: TREM2 R47H variant impairs microglial phagocytosis and clearance of amyloid-beta and apoptotic neurons. The strongest Alzheimer's risk variant discovered since APOE e4.
notes: TREM2 R47H is rare (~0.5% carrier frequency) but has strong effect (OR 2-4 for Alzheimer's). Discovered 2012-2013, confirmed the neuroinflammation hypothesis of Alzheimer's. TREM2 is now a therapeutic target.

AA.description: Reduced complement factor H function, increased complement activation and AMD susceptibility

AG.description: Intermediate CFH function, moderately increased age-related macular degeneration risk

GG.description: Normal CFH function, better complement regulation in the retina

description: CFH I62V variant modifies complement factor H function, affecting complement regulation in the retina. Secondary AMD-associated CFH variant that modifies risk in combination with Y402H.
notes: CFH I62V is in linkage disequilibrium with Y402H (rs1061170) but provides independent risk information. The combined CFH haplotype structure is important for accurate AMD risk prediction.

CC.description: More sensitive TRPV1 receptor, lower capsaicin tolerance, perceives spicy food as more intense

CT.description: Intermediate TRPV1 sensitivity, moderate capsaicin perception

TT.description: Less sensitive TRPV1 receptor, higher capsaicin tolerance, better handles spicy food

description: TRPV1 I585V variant affects the transient receptor potential vanilloid 1 channel, the primary capsaicin (chili pepper) and heat-pain receptor. Determines spice sensitivity and pain threshold.
notes: TRPV1 is activated by capsaicin, heat (>43C), and acidic pH. The I585V variant affects receptor sensitivity and desensitization kinetics. Also influences thermal pain perception and migraine susceptibility.

CC.description: Standard catalase expression, normal hydrogen peroxide detoxification

CT.description: Mildly reduced catalase promoter activity, slightly lower antioxidant defense

TT.description: Lower catalase expression, reduced hydrogen peroxide clearance capacity

description: CAT -262C>T promoter variant affects catalase expression, the primary enzyme for converting hydrogen peroxide to water and oxygen in cellular antioxidant defense.
notes: Catalase is a key antioxidant enzyme. The T allele reduces promoter activity. Combined with SOD2 and GPX1 variants, contributes to overall oxidative stress defense capacity.

CC.description: Standard complement factor I regulation, baseline macular degeneration risk

CT.description: Intermediate complement regulation, slightly elevated AMD risk

TT.description: Altered complement factor I activity, increased age-related macular degeneration susceptibility

description: CFI variant affects complement factor I, a serine protease that degrades C3b and C4b. Part of the complement pathway implicated in AMD pathogenesis.
notes: CFI joins CFH, C3, and CFB as complement pathway genes implicated in AMD. The complement system's role in drusen formation and retinal inflammation is well established.

AA.description: Altered CACNA1C calcium channel expression, associated with increased bipolar disorder and schizophrenia susceptibility

AG.description: Moderate CACNA1C effect, slightly elevated mood disorder susceptibility

GG.description: Standard CACNA1C expression, population-average bipolar disorder risk

description: CACNA1C encodes the alpha-1C subunit of L-type voltage-gated calcium channels. One of the most replicated psychiatric GWAS hits, associated with bipolar disorder and schizophrenia.
notes: CACNA1C is a cross-disorder psychiatric risk gene. The risk allele has been linked to enhanced amygdala activation and increased prefrontal cortex activity during emotional processing.

AA.description: Reduced drug efflux, higher brain and tissue drug concentrations

AG.description: Moderate drug efflux pump function, intermediate drug levels

GG.description: Increased drug efflux, lower intracellular drug concentrations, potential treatment resistance

description: ABCB1/MDR1 C3435T variant affects P-glycoprotein expression, a key drug efflux transporter at the blood-brain barrier and intestinal epithelium affecting bioavailability of many drugs.
notes: P-glycoprotein transports hundreds of drugs including chemotherapeutics, antivirals, immunosuppressants, and cardiac drugs. The T allele (A) reduces P-gp expression, increasing drug absorption and brain penetration.

CC.description: Standard SIX6 function, population-average optic nerve development and glaucoma risk

CT.description: Intermediate effect on retinal ganglion cell development, slightly elevated glaucoma susceptibility

TT.description: Altered SIX6 activity, increased optic disc cupping and primary open-angle glaucoma risk

description: SIX1/SIX6 locus variant affects retinal ganglion cell development and optic nerve head morphology. SIX6 is a homeobox transcription factor essential for eye development.
notes: SIX6 variants affect optic disc area and vertical cup-disc ratio, key endophenotypes for glaucoma. The risk allele is associated with reduced retinal nerve fiber layer thickness.

AA.description: Normal MCT1 activity, efficient lactate clearance during high-intensity exercise

AT.description: Moderately reduced MCT1 transporter function, intermediate lactate buffering

TT.description: Reduced MCT1 activity, slower lactate removal, earlier fatigue during intense exercise

description: MCT1 (SLC16A1) A1470T variant affects monocarboxylate transporter 1, the primary lactate shuttle in skeletal muscle. Influences exercise tolerance and recovery between high-intensity efforts.
notes: MCT1 transports lactate across cell membranes for use as fuel by oxidative fibers, heart, and brain. The T allele reduces transport efficiency. Important for repeated sprint and interval training performance.

CC.description: Full OGG1 glycosylase activity, efficient removal of 8-oxoguanine lesions

CG.description: Intermediate OGG1 activity, slightly reduced 8-oxoguanine repair

GG.description: Lower OGG1 efficiency, impaired 8-oxoguanine removal, modestly increased cancer susceptibility

description: OGG1 Ser326Cys variant affects 8-oxoguanine DNA glycosylase, the primary enzyme for repairing oxidative DNA damage. Impaired repair increases mutagenesis and cancer risk.
notes: 8-oxoguanine is the most common oxidative DNA lesion (~1000/cell/day). The Cys326 variant has reduced repair capacity. Particularly relevant for lung cancer risk in smokers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Standard CYP1B1 activity, normal estrogen metabolism

CG.description: Moderately increased CYP1B1 activity, slightly elevated genotoxic estrogen metabolites

GG.description: Increased CYP1B1 catalytic activity, elevated genotoxic estrogen metabolites, higher hormone-related cancer risk

description: CYP1B1 Leu432Val variant increases enzyme activity for converting estradiol to 4-hydroxyestradiol, a genotoxic catechol estrogen implicated in breast and endometrial cancer.
notes: CYP1B1 catalyzes estrogen 4-hydroxylation, producing catechol estrogens that form DNA adducts. The Val432 variant has ~3x higher catalytic efficiency for estradiol oxidation. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous PALB2 variant, significantly increased breast cancer susceptibility

AG.description: PALB2 carrier, moderately increased breast cancer risk (~2-4x lifetime risk)

GG.description: Wild-type PALB2, population-average cancer susceptibility

description: PALB2 (Partner and Localizer of BRCA2) variant impairs BRCA2-mediated homologous recombination repair. Moderate-to-high penetrance breast cancer susceptibility gene.
notes: PALB2 bridges BRCA1 and BRCA2 in the DNA repair complex. Pathogenic variants confer ~35% lifetime breast cancer risk, now classified alongside BRCA1/2 for clinical genetic testing. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Altered CDKN2A/B expression, faster cellular senescence and biological aging markers

CT.description: Moderate effect on cell cycle regulation and senescence

TT.description: Normal CDKN2A/B expression, population-average biological aging

description: CDKN2A/B locus encodes p16INK4a and p15INK4b tumor suppressors that regulate cellular senescence. Variants affect both type 2 diabetes risk and biological aging rate.
notes: 9p21 is the most replicated GWAS locus, associated with coronary disease, type 2 diabetes, and glioma. p16INK4a is a key senescence marker that accumulates with age.

CC.description: Reduced TERC expression, shorter leukocyte telomere length

CT.description: Moderate TERC activity, average telomere maintenance

TT.description: Higher TERC expression, longer leukocyte telomere length, potentially slower cellular aging

description: TERC encodes the RNA component of telomerase, essential for telomere maintenance. Variants affect leukocyte telomere length, a biomarker of biological aging.
notes: TERC provides the template for telomeric repeat addition. Along with TERT, it is a major genetic determinant of telomere length. Shorter telomeres associate with cardiovascular disease and mortality.

AA.description: Reduced CYP2R1 25-hydroxylase activity, lower circulating 25(OH)D levels

AG.description: Moderate CYP2R1 activity, intermediate vitamin D hydroxylation

GG.description: Full CYP2R1 activity, efficient conversion of vitamin D to 25(OH)D

description: CYP2R1 is the major vitamin D 25-hydroxylase that converts vitamin D to its circulating form 25(OH)D. Variants directly affect serum vitamin D levels.
notes: CYP2R1 performs the first hydroxylation step in vitamin D activation. GWAS confirmed it as one of the top genetic determinants of serum 25(OH)D levels, along with GC and DHCR7.

CC.phenotype: No Red Hair from This Variant
CC.description: Normal MC1R signaling at this position, eumelanin production unaffected

CT.description: One copy of D84E, slight shift toward pheomelanin, may lighten hair color

TT.description: Homozygous D84E, reduced eumelanin switching, contributes to red hair and fair skin phenotype

description: MC1R D84E is a loss-of-function variant reducing melanocortin-1 receptor signaling, shifting melanin production from eumelanin (brown/black) to pheomelanin (red/yellow).
notes: MC1R D84E is one of several MC1R variants contributing to red hair phenotype. Usually requires compound heterozygosity with another MC1R variant (R151C, R160W, D294H) for full red hair expression.

AA.description: Elevated HTRA1 expression, increased extracellular matrix degradation and AMD susceptibility

AG.description: Intermediate HTRA1 expression, moderately elevated AMD risk

GG.description: Normal HTRA1 promoter activity, population-average AMD risk

description: HTRA1 promoter variant increases expression of the HtrA serine peptidase, which degrades extracellular matrix components. Part of the 10q26 AMD risk locus with ARMS2.
notes: HTRA1 and ARMS2 are in strong LD at 10q26. Debate continues whether ARMS2 or HTRA1 is the causal gene. HTRA1 degrades fibronectin, elastin and proteoglycans in Bruch's membrane.

AG.description: Heterozygous ERBB2 coding variant, moderately increased breast cancer susceptibility

AA.description: Wild-type ERBB2 at this position, population-average cancer risk

GG.description: Homozygous ERBB2 variant, increased HER2 signaling and breast cancer susceptibility

description: ERBB2 (HER2) Ile655Val coding variant affects receptor tyrosine kinase activity. HER2 overexpression drives ~20% of breast cancers and is a major therapeutic target.
notes: ERBB2 I655V in the transmembrane domain affects receptor dimerization. The Val allele may enhance HER2 signaling. HER2-targeted therapies (trastuzumab, pertuzumab) have transformed HER2+ breast cancer treatment. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Enhanced Cdx2 binding, increased intestinal VDR expression and calcium absorption

AG.description: Moderate Cdx2 binding, intermediate vitamin D receptor levels

GG.description: Reduced Cdx2 transcription factor binding, lower intestinal VDR and calcium absorption

description: VDR Cdx2 polymorphism affects binding of the intestinal transcription factor Cdx2 to the VDR promoter, directly influencing intestinal calcium absorption and bone health.
notes: The A allele creates a stronger Cdx2 binding site, increasing VDR transcription in intestinal cells. This is functionally distinct from the FokI, BsmI, and TaqI VDR polymorphisms.

AA.description: Homozygous ATM variant, significantly impaired DNA damage response and elevated multi-cancer risk

AT.description: ATM carrier, mildly increased breast and other cancer risk (~2x)

TT.description: Wild-type ATM, normal DNA damage checkpoint signaling

description: ATM (Ataxia Telangiectasia Mutated) kinase is the master regulator of the DNA double-strand break response. Heterozygous carriers have moderately increased cancer risk.
notes: ATM is a serine/threonine kinase activated by DNA double-strand breaks. Homozygous loss causes ataxia-telangiectasia; heterozygous carriers (~1% of population) have ~2x breast cancer risk. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

GG.description: Normal alpha-synuclein expression, population-average Parkinson's disease risk

GT.description: Modestly increased SNCA expression, slightly elevated Parkinson's disease susceptibility

TT.description: Increased alpha-synuclein expression, higher risk of Parkinson's disease and Lewy body pathology

description: SNCA 3' region variant affects alpha-synuclein expression levels. Alpha-synuclein aggregation into Lewy bodies is the pathological hallmark of Parkinson's disease.
notes: SNCA was the first Parkinson's disease gene identified. Common variants in the 3' region modestly increase risk by elevating expression. SNCA duplications/triplications cause familial Parkinson's with gene-dosage effect.

AG.description: Heterozygous ENAM variant, slightly reduced enamel thickness or quality

AA.description: Wild-type enamelin, standard tooth enamel development

GG.description: Homozygous ENAM variant, higher risk of enamel hypoplasia and amelogenesis imperfecta

description: Enamelin (ENAM) is the largest protein in developing enamel. Variants cause autosomal dominant amelogenesis imperfecta, affecting enamel thickness and mineralization.
notes: ENAM mutations are the most common cause of autosomal dominant amelogenesis imperfecta. Enamelin is critical for enamel crystal elongation during the secretory stage of amelogenesis.

CC.description: Normal BCMO1 activity, efficient conversion of beta-carotene to retinal (vitamin A)

CT.description: ~30% reduced BCMO1 activity, less efficient beta-carotene to vitamin A conversion

TT.description: ~50% reduced BCMO1 activity, significantly less efficient provitamin A conversion, may need preformed vitamin A

description: BCMO1 (BCO1) variant reduces beta-carotene 15,15'-oxygenase activity, the enzyme that cleaves beta-carotene into retinal. Poor converters may not get adequate vitamin A from plant sources alone.
notes: ~45% of the population carries at least one reduced-function allele. Combined with rs7501331, up to 69% reduction in conversion. Important for vegans relying on plant-based vitamin A (beta-carotene).

GG.description: Lower ERCC2 helicase activity, impaired nucleotide excision repair, increased cancer susceptibility

GT.description: Moderate ERCC2 function, slightly reduced DNA repair efficiency

TT.description: Full ERCC2/XPD helicase activity, efficient nucleotide excision repair

description: ERCC2/XPD Lys751Gln variant affects the DNA helicase component of nucleotide excision repair (NER). NER removes bulky DNA adducts from UV damage and chemical carcinogens.
notes: ERCC2/XPD is part of the TFIIH transcription/repair complex. The Gln751 variant reduces repair of UV and chemical DNA damage. Particularly relevant for lung cancer risk in smokers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Baseline FOXO3 activity, population-average aging trajectory

CT.description: Intermediate FOXO3 expression, slightly enhanced stress resistance and longevity potential

TT.description: Increased FOXO3 activity, enhanced cellular stress resistance and associated with exceptional longevity

description: FOXO3 variant in the insulin/IGF-1 signaling pathway. FOXO3 transcription factor regulates stress resistance, apoptosis, and autophagy. One of the most replicated human longevity genes.
notes: FOXO3 is the only gene besides APOE consistently replicated for human longevity across multiple populations. It mediates the longevity effects of caloric restriction and insulin signaling.

AG.description: Heterozygous 8q24 risk variant, slightly increased breast cancer susceptibility

AA.description: Non-risk genotype at 8q24 locus, population-average breast cancer risk

GG.description: Homozygous 8q24 risk variant, modestly increased breast cancer susceptibility

description: 8q24 gene desert variant near MYC oncogene associated with breast cancer susceptibility through long-range enhancer regulation of MYC expression.
notes: 8q24 harbors multiple independent cancer risk loci affecting different cancer types. This variant specifically associates with breast cancer. The region contains enhancer elements that loop to contact the MYC promoter. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous 2q35 risk variant, modestly increased breast cancer susceptibility

AG.description: Heterozygous, slightly increased breast cancer risk

GG.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average breast cancer susceptibility

description: 2q35 variant in an intergenic region associated with breast cancer susceptibility. One of multiple common low-penetrance breast cancer risk loci identified by GWAS.
notes: 2q35 is one of the first GWAS-identified breast cancer susceptibility loci. Effect size is modest (OR ~1.1-1.2 per allele) but highly replicated. Risk allele frequency ~50% in Europeans. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Standard ZNF804A expression, population-average schizophrenia risk

AC.description: Intermediate ZNF804A effect, modestly increased schizophrenia susceptibility

CC.description: Altered ZNF804A expression, increased schizophrenia and bipolar disorder susceptibility

description: ZNF804A was the first gene to reach genome-wide significance for schizophrenia. The zinc finger protein affects brain connectivity, white matter integrity, and Theory of Mind processing.
notes: ZNF804A risk allele affects inter-hemispheric connectivity and prefrontal-hippocampal coupling. The variant also increases bipolar disorder risk, supporting the schizophrenia-bipolar genetic overlap.

CC.description: Standard FKBP5 expression, normal cortisol feedback and HPA axis regulation

CT.description: Moderately increased FKBP5 expression, slightly prolonged cortisol response to stress

TT.description: Increased FKBP5 expression, impaired cortisol feedback, prolonged stress response, PTSD vulnerability

description: FKBP5 co-chaperone protein regulates glucocorticoid receptor sensitivity. Variants alter HPA axis feedback, affecting cortisol response duration and susceptibility to stress-related disorders.
notes: FKBP5 T allele increases protein expression, which inhibits glucocorticoid receptor nuclear translocation, creating ultra-short negative feedback loop dysfunction. Gene x environment interaction with childhood trauma for PTSD.

AA.description: Reduced tyrosinase activity, contributes to lighter/bluer eye color

AG.description: Intermediate tyrosinase function, subtle influence toward lighter eye color

GG.description: Normal tyrosinase activity at this position, standard melanin contribution to eye color

description: TYR (tyrosinase) variant modifies eye color by affecting the rate-limiting enzyme in melanin biosynthesis. Contributes to the blue eye phenotype in combination with HERC2/OCA2.
notes: Tyrosinase catalyzes the first two steps of melanin synthesis. This variant is one of several TYR polymorphisms affecting pigmentation. Also associated with freckling and tanning response.

AA.description: Homozygous 8q24 risk variant, significantly increased prostate cancer susceptibility

AC.description: Heterozygous, moderately increased prostate cancer risk

CC.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average prostate cancer susceptibility

description: 8q24 variant associated with prostate cancer susceptibility. This gene desert region contains multiple independent prostate cancer risk loci that regulate MYC expression.
notes: One of the first GWAS-identified prostate cancer variants. Risk allele is more common in African-ancestry populations, partially explaining higher prostate cancer incidence in this group. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Normal myocilin at this position, population-average glaucoma risk

CT.description: Intermediate effect on myocilin, mildly increased intraocular pressure tendency

TT.description: Altered myocilin expression, increased risk of elevated intraocular pressure and glaucoma

description: MYOC (myocilin) variant affects the trabecular meshwork protein involved in aqueous humor outflow. MYOC mutations are the most common genetic cause of primary open-angle glaucoma.
notes: Myocilin mutations cause 3-5% of primary open-angle glaucoma cases. The protein is secreted into aqueous humor and may regulate intraocular pressure through trabecular meshwork remodeling.

AA.description: VDR BsmI BB genotype, associated with slightly lower bone mineral density

AG.description: VDR BsmI Bb genotype, average bone mineral density

GG.description: VDR BsmI bb genotype, associated with slightly higher bone mineral density

description: VDR BsmI polymorphism in intron 8 affects VDR mRNA stability and expression levels. One of the classic VDR variants studied for osteoporosis risk.
notes: BsmI is in the 3' region of VDR and affects mRNA stability through LD with a poly-A microsatellite. In LD with TaqI (rs731236) and ApaI variants. Effect on BMD is modest and population-dependent.

AA.description: Reduced OCA2/HERC2 expression, lighter iris pigmentation

AG.description: Moderate melanin in iris, intermediate eye color intensity

GG.description: Higher OCA2 expression, more intense iris pigmentation

description: OCA2/HERC2 regulatory variant affecting melanin density in the iris. Part of the multi-SNP eye color determination system on chromosome 15.
notes: Works in concert with rs12913832 (HERC2) to determine overall eye color intensity. The OCA2/HERC2 region on 15q contains the strongest eye color determinants.

CC.description: Reduced hippocampal efficiency during memory encoding, lower working memory scores

CT.description: Intermediate hippocampal activation and memory performance

TT.description: Enhanced hippocampal memory consolidation, better working memory and learning rate

description: KIBRA (WWC1) variant affects working memory and learning rate through modulation of synaptic plasticity in the hippocampus. Replicated memory-associated gene.
notes: KIBRA T allele carriers show better episodic memory performance across multiple studies. The protein interacts with PKMzeta and dendrin at postsynaptic densities, modulating long-term potentiation.

CC.description: Higher FADS2 delta-6-desaturase activity, efficient omega-3 and omega-6 processing

CT.description: Moderate FADS2 activity, intermediate fatty acid processing

TT.description: Lower FADS2 activity, less efficient fatty acid conversion, may benefit from direct EPA/DHA intake

description: FADS2 delta-6-desaturase variant affects the rate-limiting step in long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid synthesis from dietary precursors (ALA to EPA, LA to GLA).
notes: FADS2 performs the first desaturation step in both omega-3 and omega-6 pathways. The FADS1/FADS2 gene cluster shows strong signals of positive selection in populations with plant-based diets.

CC.description: Efficient FADS2 conversion, adequate brain DHA for cognitive development

CG.description: Moderate FADS2 activity, intermediate DHA availability for brain development

GG.description: Reduced FADS2 activity, lower endogenous DHA production, may benefit from direct DHA supplementation

description: FADS2 variant affecting DHA (docosahexaenoic acid) synthesis in the brain. DHA is critical for neuronal membrane fluidity, synaptogenesis, and cognitive development.
notes: DHA comprises ~40% of polyunsaturated fatty acids in the brain. This FADS2 variant interacts with breastfeeding status — the IQ benefit of breastfeeding may be modulated by infant FADS2 genotype.

CC.description: Wild-type amelogenin, standard tooth enamel formation

CT.description: Heterozygous amelogenin variant (females), mild enamel hypoplasia risk

TT.description: Homozygous or hemizygous amelogenin variant, increased risk of enamel defects

description: AMELX encodes amelogenin, the most abundant protein in developing enamel (~90%). X-linked variants cause amelogenesis imperfecta with thin or absent enamel.
notes: AMELX is X-linked, so males (hemizygous) are more severely affected. Amelogenin guides hydroxyapatite crystal formation during enamel development. Also used in forensic sex determination (AMELX vs AMELY).

CC.description: Wild-type HFE at H63D position, standard iron absorption

CG.description: HFE H63D carrier, slightly increased iron absorption, usually clinically insignificant alone

GG.description: Homozygous HFE H63D, moderately increased iron absorption, monitor ferritin levels

description: HFE H63D is a common hemochromatosis-associated variant with milder effect than C282Y. Compound heterozygosity (C282Y/H63D) can cause clinical hemochromatosis.
notes: H63D carrier frequency ~15% in Europeans. Alone, rarely causes clinical hemochromatosis. Compound heterozygosity with C282Y (~1% of population) can cause iron overload. Higher iron may benefit athletic performance.

AG.description: BRCA1 missense variant carrier, potential modifier of breast/ovarian cancer risk

AA.description: Wild-type BRCA1 at this position, standard cancer susceptibility

GG.description: Homozygous BRCA1 variant, possible effect on DNA repair efficiency

description: BRCA1 missense variant that may modify DNA repair capacity. BRCA1 is critical for homologous recombination repair of DNA double-strand breaks.
notes: BRCA1 missense variants range from benign polymorphisms to pathogenic mutations. Clinical significance depends on domain location and functional studies. Variants of uncertain significance (VUS) are common. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AG.description: Heterozygous MLH1 coding variant, potential impairment of DNA mismatch repair

AA.description: Wild-type MLH1, normal DNA mismatch repair function

GG.description: Homozygous MLH1 variant, reduced mismatch repair capacity, increased hereditary cancer risk

description: MLH1 coding variant affecting DNA mismatch repair. MLH1 deficiency causes microsatellite instability and Lynch syndrome with increased colorectal, endometrial, and other cancer risks.
notes: MLH1 I219V is a common coding variant. Lynch syndrome carriers have ~80% lifetime colorectal cancer risk. MMR-deficient tumors respond well to immune checkpoint inhibitors (anti-PD-1). Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Strongly elevated TNF-alpha, significantly increased rheumatoid arthritis and autoimmune disease risk

AG.description: Increased TNF-alpha transcription, moderately elevated inflammatory and autoimmune disease risk

GG.description: Standard TNF-alpha expression, population-average inflammatory response

description: TNF-308G>A promoter variant increases TNF-alpha expression, a master pro-inflammatory cytokine. Associated with rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, and other autoimmune conditions.
notes: TNF-alpha is a therapeutic target — anti-TNF biologics (infliximab, adalimumab, etanercept) revolutionized treatment of RA and IBD. The A allele increases transcription ~2-fold.

CC.description: Reduced IL-6 production, potentially slower inflammatory signaling and exercise recovery

CG.description: Moderate IL-6 production, balanced inflammatory and recovery response

GG.description: Higher IL-6 production, stronger exercise-induced inflammatory response, may recover faster

description: IL-6 -174G>C promoter variant affects interleukin-6 expression, a key myokine released during exercise that mediates training adaptations, glucose uptake, and recovery signaling.
notes: IL-6 has dual roles — pro-inflammatory systemically but anti-inflammatory as a myokine during exercise. The G allele increases IL-6 transcription. Associated with exercise recovery, body composition, and metabolic health.

CC.description: Increased anti-inflammatory IL-10 expression, potentially beneficial for longevity and reduced chronic inflammation

CT.description: Moderate IL-10 levels, balanced inflammatory regulation

TT.description: Reduced anti-inflammatory IL-10, potentially stronger inflammatory responses

description: IL-10 -1082A>G promoter variant affects production of interleukin-10, the most important anti-inflammatory cytokine. High IL-10 is associated with longevity and reduced age-related disease.
notes: IL-10 suppresses inflammatory cytokine production. High IL-10 producers are enriched among centenarians. The anti-inflammatory genotype may protect against atherosclerosis and neurodegeneration.

CC.description: Higher basal beta-1 receptor activity, stronger heart rate response, better beta-blocker response

CG.description: Moderate beta-1 adrenergic signaling, intermediate heart rate and beta-blocker response

GG.description: Reduced beta-1 receptor coupling, lower heart rate response, reduced beta-blocker efficacy

description: ADRB1 Arg389Gly variant in the G-protein coupling domain affects beta-1 adrenergic receptor signaling. Determines heart rate response to catecholamines and beta-blocker drug efficacy.
notes: Arg389 receptor has ~3x higher cAMP stimulation than Gly389. Arg389 homozygotes show better blood pressure response to beta-blockers (metoprolol, atenolol). Important for personalized hypertension treatment.

CC.description: Normal PPARgamma activity, standard adipogenesis and insulin sensitivity

CG.description: Mildly reduced PPARgamma activity, improved insulin sensitivity, lower type 2 diabetes risk

GG.description: Reduced PPARgamma activity, better insulin sensitivity, significantly lower type 2 diabetes risk

description: PPARG Pro12Ala variant modulates the master regulator of adipocyte differentiation. The Ala allele slightly reduces receptor activity, paradoxically improving insulin sensitivity and reducing diabetes risk.
notes: Pro12Ala was one of the first validated T2D susceptibility variants. Ala allele frequency ~12% in Europeans. PPARgamma is the target of thiazolidinedione drugs (pioglitazone). Ala reduces T2D risk by ~20%.

CC.description: Higher PPARdelta activity, improved fat burning during exercise, better endurance capacity

CT.description: Moderate PPARdelta-mediated fat metabolism during exercise

TT.description: Baseline PPARdelta activity, standard fat metabolism during exercise

description: PPARD +294T>C variant increases PPARdelta expression, enhancing fatty acid oxidation in skeletal muscle. PPARdelta regulates the metabolic switch from glucose to fat burning during exercise.
notes: PPARdelta activation increases slow-twitch muscle fiber proportion and mitochondrial biogenesis. The C allele enhances endurance capacity. PPARdelta agonists (GW501516) are banned performance-enhancing drugs.

AA.description: Increased CYP1A2 induction, very fast caffeine clearance, caffeine has short-lived effects

AC.description: Enhanced CYP1A2 inducibility, moderately fast caffeine metabolism

CC.description: Standard CYP1A2 activity at this position, typical caffeine half-life

description: CYP1A2*1F variant affects inducibility of the primary caffeine-metabolizing enzyme. Ultra-rapid metabolizers clear caffeine faster and may benefit from pre-exercise caffeine for performance.
notes: CYP1A2 metabolizes ~95% of caffeine. This variant modifies enzyme inducibility by smoking, cruciferous vegetables, and other environmental factors. Interacts with rs762551 for overall caffeine metabolism speed.

AA.description: Normal TOMM40/APOE region expression, population-average aging trajectory

AG.description: Intermediate effect on mitochondrial import and aging-related biomarkers

GG.description: Altered TOMM40 expression, associated with faster cognitive decline and biological aging markers

description: TOMM40 (translocase of outer mitochondrial membrane 40) variant near APOE affects mitochondrial protein import efficiency. Associated with biological aging rate and cognitive decline.
notes: TOMM40 is adjacent to APOE on 19q13. Debate continues whether TOMM40 has independent effects beyond APOE linkage. TOMM40 poly-T repeat length correlates with age of Alzheimer's onset.

AA.description: Modified PAX6 regulation, potentially altered corneal or lens development

AG.description: Moderate PAX6 regulatory effect on eye structure development

GG.description: Standard PAX6 expression, normal ocular development

description: PAX6 is the master control gene for eye development across all species with eyes. Variants affect corneal thickness, lens development, and ocular structure.
notes: PAX6 haploinsufficiency causes aniridia (absent iris). Common variants have subtle effects on corneal curvature, central corneal thickness, and possibly myopia. PAX6 is highly conserved across evolution.

CC.description: Increased LOXL1 effect, elevated pseudoexfoliation syndrome susceptibility

CT.description: Intermediate LOXL1 modifier effect on exfoliation syndrome

TT.description: Protective LOXL1 haplotype, reduced exfoliation syndrome risk

description: LOXL1 intronic variant modifies pseudoexfoliation syndrome risk. Third independent signal at the LOXL1 locus alongside rs1048661 and rs3825942.
notes: This intronic variant may affect LOXL1 splicing or expression levels. Pseudoexfoliation is characterized by abnormal fibrillar material accumulation in the anterior chamber, clogging the trabecular meshwork.

AA.description: Full XPC function, efficient global genomic nucleotide excision repair

AC.description: Intermediate XPC activity, slightly reduced UV damage recognition

CC.description: Lower XPC efficiency, impaired recognition of UV-induced DNA damage, increased skin aging

description: XPC Ala499Val variant affects the global genome nucleotide excision repair (GG-NER) damage sensor. XPC initiates repair of UV-induced cyclobutane pyrimidine dimers and 6-4 photoproducts.
notes: XPC is the primary UV damage sensor for transcriptionally silent DNA. Homozygous XPC loss causes xeroderma pigmentosum group C. Common variants modestly affect skin cancer risk and photoaging.

CG.description: Heterozygous C3 variant, moderately increased complement activation and AMD risk

CC.description: Normal complement C3, population-average AMD risk from this variant

GG.description: Altered complement C3 function, increased complement activation and higher AMD susceptibility

description: Complement C3 R102G variant affects the central component of all three complement activation pathways. Altered C3 contributes to retinal inflammation and drusen formation in AMD.
notes: C3 joins CFH, CFI, and CFB as complement genes in AMD. R102G affects Factor H binding, altering complement regulation. The G allele (~20% frequency in Europeans) increases AMD risk ~1.5x per allele.

CC.description: ESR1 PvuII PP genotype, associated with higher bone mineral density in some populations

CT.description: ESR1 PvuII Pp genotype, intermediate bone mineral density effect

TT.description: ESR1 PvuII pp genotype, associated with slightly lower bone mineral density and fracture risk

description: ESR1 PvuII (IVS1-397T>C) intronic variant affects estrogen receptor alpha expression. Estrogen signaling is critical for bone maintenance, and ESR1 variants modify osteoporosis risk.
notes: ESR1 PvuII and XbaI (rs9340799) are classic osteoporosis candidate variants. Meta-analyses show modest but significant effects on BMD. Effect is more pronounced in postmenopausal women not on HRT.

GG.description: Higher MDM2 expression, faster p53 degradation, reduced tumor suppression, earlier cancer onset

GT.description: Intermediate MDM2 levels, moderately increased p53 degradation

TT.description: Normal MDM2 expression, standard p53 tumor suppressor activity

description: MDM2 SNP309 T>G increases Sp1 transcription factor binding, elevating MDM2 expression. MDM2 is the primary negative regulator of p53, so higher MDM2 means less tumor suppression.
notes: MDM2 SNP309 G allele accelerates tumor formation by ~10 years in Li-Fraumeni patients. Effect is modified by estrogen — stronger in women due to estrogen-enhanced Sp1 binding. G allele frequency ~35%. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Increased aromatase activity, higher estradiol production from androgens

AG.description: Moderate aromatase activity, intermediate estrogen synthesis

GG.description: Reduced aromatase activity, lower estrogen conversion from androgens

description: CYP19A1 (aromatase) variant affects the enzyme that converts androgens to estrogens. Critical for reproductive function, bone health, and breast cancer risk.
notes: Aromatase inhibitors (letrozole, anastrozole) are first-line endocrine therapy for ER+ breast cancer. CYP19A1 variants may affect response to these drugs and natural estrogen levels.

AA.description: Homozygous PTPN22 1858T, strongly increased risk of multiple autoimmune diseases

AG.description: PTPN22 1858T carrier, ~1.5-2x increased risk of type 1 diabetes, RA, and thyroid autoimmunity

GG.description: Wild-type PTPN22, population-average autoimmune disease susceptibility

description: PTPN22 R620W is the strongest non-HLA autoimmune risk variant. Affects T-cell receptor signaling and negative regulation of T-cell activation, increasing susceptibility to multiple autoimmune diseases.
notes: PTPN22 1858T is a gain-of-function variant that increases phosphatase activity, reducing T-cell activation threshold. Shared risk variant for type 1 diabetes, RA, SLE, thyroid autoimmunity, and vitiligo.

AG.description: Heterozygous DSPP variant, possible subtle dentin quality change

AA.description: Wild-type DSPP, standard dentin mineralization and structure

GG.description: Altered DSPP expression, increased risk of dentinogenesis imperfecta

description: DSPP encodes dentin sialophosphoprotein, processed into DSP and DPP, the most abundant non-collagenous proteins in dentin. Critical for dentin mineralization and tooth structure.
notes: DSPP mutations cause dentinogenesis imperfecta types II and III, characterized by opalescent, weak teeth prone to fracture. DPP initiates dentin mineralization by nucleating hydroxyapatite crystals.

AA.description: Higher TERT expression, longer telomeres but increased multi-cancer susceptibility

AG.description: Moderate TERT expression, balanced telomere maintenance

GG.description: Baseline TERT expression and telomere maintenance

description: TERT coding region variant affecting telomerase activity. Illustrates the aging-cancer tradeoff: higher telomerase protects against senescence but enables cancer cell immortality.
notes: TERT 5p15.33 locus contains multiple independent cancer risk signals. Telomerase reactivation occurs in ~90% of human cancers. TERT promoter mutations are the most common non-coding mutations in cancer. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

GG.description: Increased FOXO3 activity, better cellular stress resistance, enriched in centenarians

GT.description: Intermediate FOXO3 expression, moderate stress resistance benefit

TT.description: Baseline FOXO3 activity, population-average aging and stress response

description: FOXO3 longevity-associated variant affecting the forkhead box O3 transcription factor. Independently replicated across Japanese, German, Italian, Chinese, and American centenarian cohorts.
notes: FOXO3 variants regulate autophagy, oxidative stress resistance, and apoptosis. The longevity allele may increase FOXO3 nuclear localization and transcriptional activity. Effect size ~1.5x for reaching 100 years.

AA.description: No *4 allele, standard CYP2C19 metabolizer status

AG.description: One *4 allele, reduced CYP2C19 function, may affect drug metabolism

GG.description: Homozygous *4, loss of CYP2C19 function, clopidogrel and PPI dose adjustment needed

description: CYP2C19*4 is a rare loss-of-function allele affecting a splice site. Contributes to poor metabolizer status for clopidogrel, proton pump inhibitors, and antidepressants.
notes: CYP2C19*4 is rare (<1% in most populations) but clinically significant. Together with *2 and *3, accounts for >95% of CYP2C19 loss-of-function alleles worldwide.

CC.description: Normal FGFR2 regulation, population-average breast cancer risk

CT.description: Intermediate FGFR2 expression, ~1.2x increased breast cancer risk

TT.description: Higher FGFR2 expression, ~1.4x increased ER-positive breast cancer risk

description: FGFR2 intron 2 variant increases fibroblast growth factor receptor 2 expression, the strongest common breast cancer susceptibility locus identified by GWAS.
notes: FGFR2 is the strongest common breast cancer risk locus (OR ~1.26 per allele). Risk allele frequency ~38%. Effect is stronger for ER-positive breast cancer. FGFR2 amplification also drives some cancers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AG.description: HLA-DRB1*1501 tag carrier, ~3x increased multiple sclerosis risk

AA.description: No HLA-DRB1*1501 risk allele, population-average MS risk

GG.description: Homozygous HLA-DRB1*1501 tag, ~6x increased multiple sclerosis risk

description: HLA-DRB1*1501 tag SNP is the strongest genetic risk factor for multiple sclerosis. The MHC class II molecule presents myelin-derived peptides, potentially triggering autoimmune demyelination.
notes: HLA-DRB1*1501 confers the largest single-gene effect on MS risk. Interacts with EBV infection (nearly all MS patients are EBV+), vitamin D deficiency, and smoking. Risk allele frequency ~14% in Europeans.

AA.description: LIN28B variant associated with later onset of puberty and menarche by several months

AC.description: Intermediate LIN28B effect on pubertal timing

CC.description: Standard LIN28B expression, tendency toward earlier menarche onset

description: LIN28B variant affects pubertal timing through regulation of the let-7 microRNA family. LIN28B controls the transition from juvenile to adult gene expression programs.
notes: LIN28B was among the first GWAS hits for menarche timing. Each allele shifts menarche by ~0.1-0.2 years. LIN28B also affects adult height through its role in growth tempo regulation.

AA.description: Increased alpha-synuclein levels, elevated Parkinson's disease and Lewy body risk

AG.description: Moderate alpha-synuclein levels, slightly elevated PD risk

GG.description: Normal alpha-synuclein expression, population-average Parkinson's risk

description: SNCA 3' UTR variant modulates alpha-synuclein mRNA stability and expression. Gene dosage is critical — duplications and triplications cause familial Parkinson's with increasing severity.
notes: Alpha-synuclein aggregation into Lewy bodies is the hallmark of PD pathology. Even modest expression increases (~1.5x) from common variants contribute to population PD risk.

CT.description: GBA carrier, ~5x increased Parkinson's disease risk, earlier onset tendency

CC.description: Wild-type GBA, population-average Parkinson's disease risk

TT.description: Homozygous GBA variant, Gaucher disease and very high Parkinson's risk

description: GBA encodes glucocerebrosidase, a lysosomal enzyme. GBA mutations are the most common genetic risk factor for Parkinson's disease, linking lysosomal dysfunction to alpha-synuclein aggregation.
notes: GBA mutations are found in 5-15% of PD patients. Homozygous mutations cause Gaucher disease. GBA-PD has earlier onset and faster cognitive decline. GCase activation is a therapeutic strategy.

GG.description: Standard CYP2B6 activity, normal efavirenz and methadone metabolism

GT.description: Reduced CYP2B6 activity, slightly elevated drug levels, may need dose adjustment

TT.description: Significantly reduced CYP2B6 activity, high risk of efavirenz CNS toxicity, slow methadone metabolism

description: CYP2B6*6 (Q172H + K262R) is the most common reduced-function allele. Critical for metabolism of efavirenz (HIV), methadone (opioid maintenance), and bupropion (smoking cessation).
notes: CYP2B6*6 frequency ~25% globally, up to 50% in some African populations. TT genotype causes efavirenz plasma levels 3-4x higher than GG, increasing CNS side effects (vivid dreams, dizziness).

AA.description: Reduced SOST expression, less inhibition of bone formation, potentially higher bone density

AG.description: Moderate SOST expression, average bone formation regulation

GG.description: Standard SOST expression, normal Wnt-mediated bone formation inhibition

description: SOST encodes sclerostin, a Wnt pathway inhibitor secreted by osteocytes that suppresses bone formation. Anti-sclerostin antibodies (romosozumab) are a breakthrough osteoporosis treatment.
notes: Loss-of-function SOST mutations cause sclerosteosis (extremely dense bones). This validated sclerostin as a drug target. Romosozumab (anti-sclerostin) increases bone density more than any other osteoporosis drug.

CT.description: Heterozygous TOX3 risk variant, moderately increased breast cancer susceptibility

CC.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average breast cancer risk

TT.description: Homozygous TOX3 risk variant, ~1.4x increased breast cancer risk

description: TOX3/TNRC9 16q12 variant is one of the strongest common breast cancer susceptibility loci. TOX3 is a chromatin remodeling factor affecting estrogen-responsive gene regulation.
notes: TOX3 variant has stronger effect in ER-positive breast cancer. Risk allele frequency ~25% in Europeans. TOX3 regulates chromatin structure at estrogen-responsive enhancers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous 8q24 risk variant, increased prostate cancer susceptibility

AG.description: Heterozygous, moderately increased prostate cancer risk

GG.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average prostate cancer risk at this locus

description: Second independent 8q24 prostate cancer risk variant. The 8q24 region contains multiple independent cancer risk loci in a gene desert that regulate distant MYC oncogene expression.
notes: 8q24 contains at least 5 independent prostate cancer risk signals. Combined, they contribute substantially to familial relative risk. Risk allele frequencies vary across ancestral populations. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Protective genotype, reduced prostate cancer but slightly increased endometrial cancer risk

AG.description: Moderate HNF1B effect on cancer susceptibility

GG.description: Higher prostate cancer risk, but potentially lower endometrial cancer risk

description: HNF1B (TCF2) variant with opposing effects on prostate and endometrial cancer risk. HNF1B is a transcription factor critical for epithelial differentiation in the prostate and uterus.
notes: HNF1B shows pleiotropic opposing effects — the same allele that increases prostate cancer risk decreases endometrial cancer risk. Also associated with type 2 diabetes risk. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous TMCO1 risk variant, significantly increased primary open-angle glaucoma susceptibility

AG.description: Heterozygous, moderately elevated intraocular pressure and glaucoma risk

GG.description: Normal TMCO1 function, population-average glaucoma risk

description: TMCO1 transmembrane protein variant is one of the strongest GWAS hits for primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG) and intraocular pressure. TMCO1 may regulate calcium homeostasis in the eye.
notes: TMCO1 is a transmembrane and coiled-coil domain protein involved in ER calcium leak. Glaucoma is the leading cause of irreversible blindness, affecting ~80 million people worldwide.

AA.description: Increased FKBP5 expression, prolonged cortisol exposure, elevated PTSD vulnerability

AG.description: Moderate FKBP5 effect on glucocorticoid receptor feedback

GG.description: Standard FKBP5 levels, normal HPA axis negative feedback

description: FKBP5 variant modifying glucocorticoid receptor sensitivity and HPA axis feedback. Interacts with childhood trauma to increase PTSD and depression risk through epigenetic mechanisms.
notes: FKBP5 risk alleles create a gene-environment feedback loop: childhood trauma demethylates FKBP5, increasing expression, impairing cortisol feedback, and sensitizing to future stress. Epigenetic mechanism confirmed.

CC.description: Efficient mitochondrial import of MnSOD, better superoxide radical defense

CT.description: Moderate mitochondrial MnSOD levels, intermediate antioxidant defense

TT.description: Reduced mitochondrial MnSOD import, lower superoxide defense, potential oxidative stress

description: SOD2 Ala16Val variant affects mitochondrial targeting sequence of manganese superoxide dismutase, the primary mitochondrial antioxidant enzyme. Ala16 allows efficient mitochondrial import.
notes: The Ala variant forms an alpha-helix allowing efficient mitochondrial import. Val forms a beta-sheet, reducing import by 30-40%. Paradoxically, higher SOD2 activity has been linked to both protection and risk in different contexts.

AA.description: Non-risk genotype at 9p21 locus, baseline coronary heart disease risk

AG.description: One risk allele, ~25% increased coronary heart disease risk

GG.description: Homozygous 9p21 risk, ~50% increased coronary heart disease risk independent of lipids

description: 9p21.3 variant near CDKN2A/2B is one of the most replicated coronary heart disease risk loci, acting through ANRIL long non-coding RNA regulation of vascular cell proliferation.
notes: Third independent 9p21 variant in our panel (with rs10757274, rs1333049). Risk is independent of LDL, blood pressure, and diabetes — operates through vascular inflammation and cell cycle control.

AC.description: Intermediate AGTR1 expression, slightly elevated blood pressure tendency

AA.description: Normal angiotensin II type 1 receptor expression, standard blood pressure regulation

CC.description: Increased AGTR1 expression, enhanced angiotensin II signaling, higher hypertension risk

description: AGTR1 +1166A>C variant in the 3' UTR affects angiotensin II type 1 receptor expression. The AT1 receptor mediates vasoconstriction, sodium retention, and cardiac remodeling.
notes: The C allele disrupts a miR-155 binding site, increasing AGTR1 mRNA stability. ARBs (losartan, valsartan) block AT1 receptors regardless of genotype. C allele frequency ~25% in Europeans.

CC.description: Standard Kir6.2 KATP channel function, normal glucose-stimulated insulin secretion

CT.description: Intermediate KATP channel sensitivity, slightly reduced insulin response to glucose

TT.description: Altered KATP channel gating, impaired glucose-stimulated insulin secretion, increased T2D risk

description: KCNJ11 E23K variant affects the Kir6.2 subunit of the pancreatic beta-cell KATP channel. This channel couples glucose metabolism to insulin secretion by depolarizing the beta cell.
notes: K23 (T allele) increases KATP channel open probability, reducing insulin secretion. Sulfonylurea drugs (glyburide) close KATP channels to stimulate insulin release. Neonatal diabetes from KCNJ11 mutations responds to sulfonylureas.

AA.description: Standard GJD2/connexin-36 function, baseline myopia susceptibility

AG.description: Intermediate GJD2 effect on retinal signaling, slightly elevated myopia risk

GG.description: Altered retinal gap junction signaling, increased myopia susceptibility

description: GJD2 (connexin-36) variant affects gap junction coupling between retinal neurons. Altered retinal signaling may affect emmetropization (eye growth calibration) and myopia development.
notes: GJD2 was one of the first GWAS hits for myopia/refractive error. Connexin-36 gap junctions couple AII amacrine cells in the retina, critical for rod pathway signaling. Myopia affects ~1.5 billion people.

CC.description: PLA2/PLA2 genotype, enhanced platelet aggregation, may have reduced aspirin response

CT.description: PLA1/PLA2 heterozygous, moderate platelet aggregation

TT.description: PLA1/PLA1 genotype, standard platelet aggregation and aspirin response

description: ITGB3 Leu33Pro (HPA-1/PLA) variant affects platelet glycoprotein IIIa, the fibrinogen receptor. PLA2 (Pro33) enhances platelet aggregation and may reduce aspirin efficacy.
notes: PLA2 allele frequency ~15% in Europeans. Associated with aspirin resistance, coronary stent thrombosis, and neonatal alloimmune thrombocytopenia (NAIT). Important for antiplatelet therapy decisions.

AA.description: More sensitive FSH receptor, lower FSH needed for follicular response, better IVF response

AG.description: Moderate FSH receptor sensitivity, standard ovarian response to gonadotropins

GG.description: Less sensitive FSH receptor, higher FSH levels needed, may need higher IVF gonadotropin doses

description: FSHR Asn680Ser variant affects FSH receptor sensitivity in ovarian granulosa cells. Determines ovarian response to gonadotropins and is critical for IVF protocol optimization.
notes: Ser680 variant increases FSH threshold for follicular recruitment. Clinically used to personalize IVF gonadotropin dosing — Ser/Ser women typically need higher doses. Also affects age at menopause.

CC.description: Non-risk genotype at 15q14, baseline refractive error

CT.description: Intermediate effect on eye growth and refractive error

TT.description: Risk genotype, increased susceptibility to myopia and negative refractive error

description: 15q14 locus variant near GJD2 associated with refractive error and myopia susceptibility. One of the first GWAS-identified common myopia risk variants.
notes: Part of the 15q14 myopia susceptibility region near GJD2. Myopia prevalence has doubled in one generation in East Asia (now 80-90% in young adults), suggesting strong gene-environment interaction.

AG.description: MLH1 pathogenic variant carrier, high lifetime risk of colorectal, endometrial, and other cancers

AA.description: Wild-type MLH1, normal mismatch repair function

GG.description: Homozygous MLH1 pathogenic variant, very high multi-cancer susceptibility

description: MLH1 pathogenic variant causing Lynch syndrome (hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer). MLH1 is a key DNA mismatch repair protein; deficiency causes microsatellite instability.
notes: Lynch syndrome carriers have ~80% lifetime CRC risk, ~60% endometrial cancer risk, and elevated ovarian, gastric, and urinary tract cancer risk. MSI-high tumors respond to anti-PD-1 immunotherapy. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous protective CFB variant, significantly reduced complement activation and AMD risk

AG.description: CFB protective allele carrier, reduced complement activation and lower AMD risk

GG.description: Standard CFB function, population-average AMD risk from this variant

description: CFB R32Q protective variant reduces complement factor B activity in the alternative complement pathway. One of the first protective AMD variants identified in the complement system.
notes: CFB R32Q reduces factor B activity by ~50%, dampening alternative pathway complement activation. Protective effect (OR ~0.4) is among the strongest genetic effects in AMD. Rare protective allele (~5%).

CT.description: HLA-DQB1*0602 tag carrier, significantly increased narcolepsy type 1 susceptibility

CC.description: No HLA-DQB1*0602 risk allele, very low narcolepsy risk

TT.description: Homozygous HLA-DQB1*0602 tag, highest genetic risk for narcolepsy type 1

description: HLA-DQB1*0602 tag SNP is the strongest genetic determinant of narcolepsy type 1 (with cataplexy). Present in >98% of narcolepsy type 1 patients, enabling autoimmune destruction of hypocretin neurons.
notes: HLA-DQB1*0602 is necessary but not sufficient for narcolepsy (~25% of population carries it, but narcolepsy prevalence is ~0.05%). Narcolepsy type 1 is now considered autoimmune destruction of hypocretin/orexin neurons.

AA.description: Non-functional OR5A1 receptor, unable to detect violet/berry aroma (beta-ionone)

AG.description: Intermediate OR5A1 function, can detect beta-ionone at higher concentrations

GG.description: Fully functional OR5A1, highly sensitive to violet, floral, and berry aromas (beta-ionone)

description: OR5A1 variant determines ability to perceive beta-ionone, the characteristic scent of violets, berries, and many flowers. One of the best-characterized human olfactory receptor variants.
notes: Beta-ionone is found in roses, violets, raspberries, and many foods. ~50% of people are sensitive to beta-ionone. The variant explains why some people love floral perfumes while others find them imperceptible.

CC.description: Higher PON1 paraoxonase activity, better LDL oxidation protection, reduced CVD risk

CT.description: Moderate paraoxonase activity, intermediate cardiovascular protection

TT.description: Lower PON1 activity, reduced protection against LDL oxidation, slightly higher CVD risk

description: PON1 Q192R variant affects paraoxonase 1 enzyme activity. PON1 is an HDL-associated enzyme that prevents LDL oxidation, a key step in atherosclerosis development.
notes: PON1 is secreted by the liver and associates with HDL particles. The R192 variant has higher paraoxonase activity but lower lactonase activity — the clinical net effect depends on the substrate. Also detoxifies organophosphate pesticides.

CC.description: Standard apolipoprotein B function, population-average LDL levels

CT.description: Intermediate APOB effect, mildly higher LDL cholesterol levels

TT.description: Altered APOB expression, higher LDL cholesterol levels and cardiovascular risk

description: APOB variant affecting apolipoprotein B-100, the structural protein of LDL particles. Each LDL particle contains exactly one APOB molecule, making APOB a direct measure of atherogenic particle number.
notes: APOB is the ligand for LDL receptor-mediated clearance. APOB mutations cause familial hypobetalipoproteinemia (low) or familial defective APOB (high LDL). APOB is now considered a better CVD predictor than LDL-C.

AA.description: Increased nevus (mole) count and elevated melanoma susceptibility

AG.description: Moderate effect on mole count and melanoma risk

GG.description: Fewer nevi, baseline melanoma risk from this variant

description: MTAP 9p21 variant affects melanocyte proliferation and nevus formation. Nevus count is the strongest single risk factor for melanoma, and this variant modulates both.
notes: MTAP is adjacent to CDKN2A, the familial melanoma gene. Total nevus count >100 increases melanoma risk ~7x. This variant explains ~1% of nevus count variation but has strong epidemiological relevance.

GG.description: Higher vitamin D binding protein, more total 25(OH)D but similar bioavailable vitamin D

GT.description: Moderate vitamin D binding protein levels

TT.description: Lower vitamin D binding protein, lower total 25(OH)D but similar bioavailable vitamin D

description: GC (group-specific component) encodes vitamin D binding protein, the primary carrier of 25(OH)D in blood. Variants affect total vitamin D measurements but may not change bioavailable vitamin D.
notes: GC is the strongest genetic determinant of total serum 25(OH)D. However, if free/bioavailable vitamin D is the active form, GC genotype may be less clinically relevant. The Gc2 allele is more common in darker-skinned populations.

CC.description: Impaired WNT10A signaling, increased risk of missing teeth (tooth agenesis) and ectodermal features

CT.description: Reduced WNT10A activity, slightly increased risk of congenitally missing teeth

TT.description: Standard WNT10A function, normal tooth development

description: WNT10A is the most common genetic cause of non-syndromic tooth agenesis (congenitally missing teeth). WNT10A signals through the canonical Wnt pathway critical for ectodermal organ development.

sources.name: van den Boogaard MJ et al. (2012)

notes: WNT10A mutations account for ~50% of isolated tooth agenesis cases. Heterozygous carriers often have fewer wisdom teeth or small lateral incisors. Biallelic mutations cause ectodermal dysplasia syndromes.

CC.description: Altered VDR mRNA stability, slightly reduced vitamin D receptor expression and calcium absorption

CT.description: Intermediate VDR expression, average calcium metabolism

TT.description: Higher VDR mRNA stability, better calcium absorption and bone mineral density

description: VDR TaqI polymorphism in exon 9 is a synonymous variant affecting mRNA stability through 3' UTR haplotype structure. Part of the classic VDR polymorphism panel for bone health.
notes: TaqI is in strong LD with BsmI (rs1544410) and poly-A length variation. Despite being synonymous, affects VDR expression via mRNA stability. Effect on BMD is modest and population-dependent.

AC.description: Myocilin Q368X carrier, significantly increased risk of juvenile or adult-onset open-angle glaucoma

AA.description: Homozygous myocilin Q368X, very high risk of early-onset glaucoma

CC.description: Wild-type myocilin, population-average glaucoma risk at this position

description: MYOC Q368X is a pathogenic nonsense mutation causing misfolded myocilin protein accumulation in trabecular meshwork cells, leading to increased intraocular pressure and glaucoma.
notes: Q368X is the most common MYOC mutation (~1.6% of POAG patients). Causes autosomal dominant glaucoma with high penetrance. Misfolded myocilin is retained in ER, triggering cell death in trabecular meshwork.

CC.description: Normal BCO1 cleavage activity, efficient conversion of beta-carotene to retinal

CT.description: ~30% reduced BCO1 activity, less efficient beta-carotene to vitamin A conversion

TT.description: ~50% reduced BCO1 activity, poor beta-carotene conversion, may need preformed vitamin A sources

description: BCMO1 (BCO1) A379V variant reduces beta-carotene oxygenase activity. Combined with rs12934922, determines overall efficiency of plant-derived vitamin A production.
notes: Combined with rs12934922, up to 69% reduction in beta-carotene conversion in double homozygotes. Important for vegans and populations relying on plant-based vitamin A. ~25% carry at least one reduced-function allele.

CT.description: GBA variant carrier, ~3-5x increased PD risk, earlier onset and more rapid cognitive decline

CC.description: Homozygous GBA variant, Gaucher disease risk and very high Parkinson's susceptibility

TT.description: Wild-type GBA, population-average Parkinson's disease risk

description: GBA variant linking Gaucher disease and Parkinson's disease. Demonstrates the connection between lysosomal dysfunction and alpha-synuclein aggregation in neurodegeneration.
notes: GBA mutations impair glucocerebrosidase, causing glucocerebroside accumulation that promotes alpha-synuclein aggregation. GBA-targeted therapies (ambroxol, venglustat) are in clinical trials for Parkinson's.

CC.description: Full CYP3A5 expression, rapid tacrolimus metabolism, may need higher doses

CT.description: Reduced CYP3A5 expression, intermediate tacrolimus metabolism

TT.description: No CYP3A5 expression (cryptic splice site), slower tacrolimus metabolism, lower doses needed

description: CYP3A5*3 splice variant determines CYP3A5 expression. Critical for transplant immunosuppression dosing — tacrolimus dose requirements differ 1.5-2x between expressors and non-expressors.
notes: *3 creates a cryptic splice site that abolishes CYP3A5 expression. ~90% of Europeans are *3/*3 non-expressors, vs ~30% of Africans. CPIC recommends genotype-guided tacrolimus dosing in transplant patients.

AT.description: Moderate CNTNAP2 effect on language development and neural connectivity

AA.description: Standard CNTNAP2 function, typical language and social communication development

TT.description: Altered CNTNAP2 expression, increased risk of language delay and autism spectrum traits

description: CNTNAP2 (contactin-associated protein-like 2) variant affects neuronal connectivity and language development. One of the strongest candidate genes for language disorders and autism spectrum conditions.
notes: CNTNAP2 is one of the largest genes in the human genome (2.3Mb). It clusters potassium channels at nodes of Ranvier. Disruptions affect language lateralization, frontal lobe connectivity, and social cognition.

AA.description: FTO risk variant, ~1.5kg/allele higher body weight, increased type 2 diabetes susceptibility

AC.description: Intermediate FTO effect, modest increase in BMI and diabetes risk

CC.description: Non-risk FTO genotype, population-average BMI and diabetes risk

description: FTO (fat mass and obesity-associated) variant affects IRX3/IRX5 regulation through long-range enhancer activity, influencing adipocyte thermogenesis and energy expenditure.
notes: FTO was the first obesity GWAS locus (2007). Despite the name, the causal mechanism acts through IRX3/IRX5 via chromatin looping, switching adipocyte program from energy-burning to energy-storing.

AA.description: Normal bilirubin glucuronidation, standard irinotecan metabolism

AT.description: Mildly reduced glucuronidation, slightly elevated bilirubin and irinotecan toxicity risk

TT.description: Significantly reduced UGT1A1 activity, elevated bilirubin (Gilbert syndrome), high irinotecan toxicity risk

description: UGT1A1*28 TA repeat variant reduces UGT1A1 expression, causing Gilbert syndrome (benign unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia) and increasing irinotecan chemotherapy toxicity risk.
notes: UGT1A1*28 (7 TA repeats vs 6 in *1) reduces transcription by ~70%. Gilbert syndrome affects 5-10% of population. FDA label for irinotecan recommends UGT1A1 genotyping. *28/*28 patients need 30% dose reduction.

AA.description: Higher hepcidin levels from TMPRSS6 variant, reduced intestinal iron absorption, lower iron stores

AG.description: Moderate TMPRSS6 function, intermediate hepcidin and iron regulation

GG.description: Full TMPRSS6/matriptase-2 function, normal hepcidin suppression and iron absorption

description: TMPRSS6 A736V variant affects matriptase-2, a protease that cleaves hemojuvelin to suppress hepcidin (the master iron regulatory hormone). Reduced TMPRSS6 means higher hepcidin and lower iron absorption.
notes: TMPRSS6 is the strongest GWAS hit for serum iron, hemoglobin, and transferrin saturation. The A allele raises hepcidin, reducing iron absorption. Relevant for iron deficiency anemia susceptibility.

CC.description: Homozygous protective C2 variant, significantly reduced complement activation and AMD risk

CG.description: C2 protective allele carrier, reduced classical complement pathway and lower AMD risk

GG.description: Standard C2 function, population-average AMD risk from this variant

description: Complement C2 protective variant reduces classical complement pathway activation in the retina. Identified alongside CFB as a protective factor against AMD.
notes: C2 and CFB are adjacent in the MHC class III region on chromosome 6. Together they demonstrate that reduced complement activation protects against AMD, validating complement-targeted AMD therapies.

AA.description: ESR1 XbaI XX genotype, associated with slightly higher bone mineral density

AG.description: ESR1 XbaI Xx genotype, intermediate bone mineral density and fracture risk

GG.description: ESR1 XbaI xx genotype, associated with lower bone mineral density and increased fracture risk

description: ESR1 XbaI (IVS1-351A>G) intronic variant modifies estrogen receptor alpha expression. Together with PvuII (rs2234693), forms the classic ESR1 osteoporosis haplotype.
notes: XbaI and PvuII are in LD and form haplotypes. The 'px' haplotype (low-expression) is associated with lower BMD and increased fracture risk. Effect is most pronounced in postmenopausal women.

CG.description: Intermediate effect, moderately elevated postprandial triglyceride response to dietary fat

CC.description: Standard triglyceride metabolism, normal postprandial lipemia

GG.description: Significantly higher triglyceride response to fat intake, increased cardiovascular risk

description: ZPR1/APOA5 variant affects triglyceride metabolism and postprandial lipemia. APOA5 regulates triglyceride-rich lipoprotein clearance by activating lipoprotein lipase.
notes: This locus has one of the largest effects on triglyceride levels from GWAS. Risk allele carriers show ~20% higher fasting triglycerides. APOA5 activates LPL-mediated triglyceride clearance from VLDL and chylomicrons.
